section.heading: Prohibitions on discrimination

subsection.heading: Persons in Air Transportation.—
subsection.content: An air carrier or foreign air carrier may not subject a person in air transportation to discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, or ancestry.

subsection.heading: Use of Private Airports.—
subsection.content: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no State or local government may prohibit the use or full enjoyment of a private airport within its jurisdiction by any person on the basis of that person’s race, color, national origin, religion, sex, or ancestry.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 106–181, title VII, § 706(a), Apr. 5, 2000, 114 Stat. 157.)

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section applicable only to fiscal years beginning after Sept. 30, 1999, see section 3 of Pub. L. 106–181, set out as an Effective Date of 2000 Amendments note under section 106 of this title.

note.heading: Training Policies Regarding Racial, Ethnic, and Religious Nondiscrimination
